ARC01.blurb:
Qualification rounds set the field but aren't ticketed — archers shoot 72 arrows at 70 meters to rank for elimination brackets. Women's compound debuts at the 2028 Games alongside the familiar men's recurve discipline.

Compound bows use sights and release aids for laser precision; recurve is the traditional form you recognize from historical imagery. Rankings from these sessions determine matchups through the knockout rounds.

These early rounds happen at Dignity Health Sports Park in Carson, about 13 miles south of downtown — a temporary stadium setup where archers face the target in dead silence.

ARC02.blurb:
The afternoon qualification session completes the opening round — men's compound and women's recurve archers each fire 72 arrows to establish their bracket positions. Not ticketed, but these scores matter: a strong qualifying rank can mean avoiding the top seed until later.

Compound archery makes its Games debut in 2028 with technology-assisted accuracy. Women's recurve is the traditional discipline where Korea's Lim Si-hyeon took Paris gold.

Carson Stadium at Dignity Health Sports Park hosts all archery — a venue built for precision and focus, with sightlines down the 70-meter range.

ARC03.blurb:
Compound mixed team crowns the first-ever archery champions in this format — pairs of one man and one woman shooting alternating arrows through knockout rounds all in one night. Technology-assisted bows, pinpoint accuracy, and a brand-new event.

The session runs from round of 16 through the gold medal match. Countries likely to field strong compound pairs include South Korea and the USA, both with deep rosters in World Archery rankings.

Evening at Carson Stadium — lights, silence between shots, and the tension of elimination archery where every arrow decides whether you advance or go home.
ARC03.potentialContendersIntro: Projected contenders for compound mixed team — a new event with no prior Games results but known strength in World Archery competition.

potentialContenders.name: South Korea
potentialContenders.note: Leads compound results in World Archery events; expected to field a top-ranked mixed pair.

potentialContenders.name: USA
potentialContenders.note: Strong compound program with national tournament depth; likely contenders for medals.

ARC04.blurb:
The recurve individual brackets open with round of 32 elimination — both men's and women's fields trimmed in head-to-head matches. Archers shoot sets of arrows; best of five sets wins. Upsets happen early.

South Korea enters as the dominant force: Lim Si-hyeon won Paris women's gold, Kim Woo-jin anchored the men's team to Paris gold. USA's Brady Ellison, a four-time Games veteran, could appear if he qualifies — a morning session at home would be a capstone moment.

Carson Stadium morning session — the crowd hasn't filled yet, but the archers have already been shooting since dawn in practice. The range feels wide open under Southern California sky.
ARC04.potentialContendersIntro: Likely field for recurve individual round of 32 — mix of Paris medalists and projected qualifiers.

potentialContenders.name: Lim Si-hyeon
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's individual gold medalist; South Korea's current leader.

potentialContenders.name: Kim Woo-jin
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's team gold; consistent top-level performer.

potentialContenders.name: Brady Ellison
potentialContenders.note: Four-time Games competitor; fifth in Paris men's individual; home Games opportunity.

ARC05.blurb:
Evening elimination session continues the individual recurve brackets — round of 32 wraps up, round of 16 begins. Under stadium lights the tension sharpens; every match is sudden-death archery.

South Korea's production line of recurve champions could field multiple archers deep in both draws. Brady Ellison of the USA, if competing, brings veteran presence in front of a home crowd.

Night archery at Carson Stadium — lights isolate the range, crowd noise drops to nothing between arrows, and the targets 70 meters downrange look impossibly small.
ARC05.potentialContendersIntro: Projected contenders advancing through round of 32 and 16 eliminations.

potentialContenders.name: Lim Si-hyeon
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's gold; expected to advance if she competes.

potentialContenders.name: Kim Woo-jin
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 team gold; South Korea's men's recurve anchor.

potentialContenders.name: Brady Ellison
potentialContenders.note: USA's top recurve archer; four Games of experience.

ARC06.blurb:
Sunday morning continues the grind through recurve individual brackets — more round of 32 and round of 16 matches whittling both fields toward quarterfinals. Head-to-head sets, best of five, no margin for error.

Korea's depth means multiple archers likely in the mix. An San, the Tokyo triple gold medalist, did not qualify for Paris — if she returns for 2028, this would be her stage.

Carson Stadium mid-morning — the sun's already warm, the range is busy, and the elimination pressure mounts with every session.

ARC07.blurb:
Sunday night wraps the opening weekend of individual recurve eliminations — final round of 32 and round of 16 matches before quarterfinals. The field is narrowing fast.

South Korea's men and women both dominated Paris recurve; if Kim Woo-jin or Lim Si-hyeon compete, expect them deep in the bracket. Brady Ellison, a four-Games veteran, could bring home-crowd energy to USA's men's draw.

Evening session at Carson Stadium — the weekend crowd fills the stands, and the elimination stakes make every arrow count.
ARC07.potentialContendersIntro: Expected archers advancing through Sunday evening eliminations in recurve individual.

potentialContenders.name: Kim Woo-jin
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's team gold; South Korea's recurve anchor.

potentialContenders.name: Lim Si-hyeon
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's individual and team gold.

potentialContenders.name: Brady Ellison
potentialContenders.note: Four-time Games participant; home venue advantage.

ARC08.blurb:
Monday morning focuses solely on women's recurve — round of 32 and round of 16 matches thinning the field before quarterfinals. Single-elimination archery rewards consistency and nerve.

South Korea swept Paris women's recurve team gold with Lim Si-hyeon leading. If she competes, she's the favorite. An San's Tokyo heroics still echo, but her absence from Paris shows how brutal Korea's trials are.

Carson Stadium mid-morning — a quieter session than evening finals, but the arrows still fly at 70 meters and every set matters.

potentialContenders.name: Lim Si-hyeon

ARC09.blurb:
Men's recurve team medals are decided in one night — quarterfinals through gold. Three archers per team, alternating shots, elimination rounds building to the final. South Korea won Paris team gold with Kim Woo-jin and is the clear favorite if they field a similar squad.

USA men's team placed outside medals at Paris but could be stronger at home. Brady Ellison, if competing, brings veteran leadership to a team event that rewards coordination and composure.

Evening at Carson Stadium — team archery has a different rhythm than individual, with strategy between partners and the crowd reacting to every set.
ARC09.potentialContendersIntro: Expected teams competing in men's recurve team finals — South Korea dominates, but other nations project to challenge.

potentialContenders.name: South Korea
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's team gold with Kim Woo-jin, Lee Woo-seok, Kim Je-deok; heavy favorites.

potentialContenders.name: USA
potentialContenders.note: Could feature Brady Ellison; home venue advantage and improving depth.

ARC10.blurb:
Tuesday morning continues men's recurve individual eliminations — round of 32 and round of 16 matches narrowing the men's bracket. Head-to-head sets, five arrows per set, best of five.

Kim Woo-jin won Paris team gold; Lee Woo-seok took Paris individual gold. Korea's men's recurve depth is extraordinary. Brady Ellison, if he competes, would carry USA hopes in a home Games.

Carson Stadium mid-morning session — the range is set, the targets are 70 meters downrange, and the eliminations are relentless.
ARC10.potentialContendersIntro: Projected men's recurve competitors in Tuesday eliminations — Paris medalists and likely qualifiers.

potentialContenders.name: Kim Woo-jin

ARC11.blurb:
Women's recurve team medals are on the line — quarterfinals, semifinals, bronze, and gold all in one evening. South Korea won Paris team gold with Lim Si-hyeon, Nam Su-hyeon, and Jeong Dasomi and remains the program to beat. No other nation matches Korea's recurve depth.

Team eliminations are best-of-four sets, six arrows per set, three archers rotating. Strategy and execution under maximum pressure.

Tuesday night at Carson Stadium — the crowd fills for a medal session, and the women's team final could be Korea versus the field.
ARC11.potentialContendersIntro: Expected teams in women's recurve team finals — South Korea dominated Paris and projects to lead again.

potentialContenders.name: South Korea
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's team gold; Lim Si-hyeon and teammates dominated.

ARC12.blurb:
Mixed team recurve begins eliminations — round of 16 matches pairing one man and one woman per country, alternating arrows. South Korea won Paris mixed gold with Lim Si-hyeon and Kim Woo-jin; if both compete, they're the duo to beat.

Brady Ellison could anchor a USA mixed pair in front of a home crowd. The format rewards coordination and composure under shared pressure.

Wednesday late morning at Carson Stadium — mixed team is a newer event, but the archery fundamentals don't change: 70 meters, dead silence, arrows in the gold.
ARC12.potentialContendersIntro: Projected mixed team pairs in recurve round of 16 — Paris medalists and likely qualifiers.

potentialContenders.name: Lim Si-hyeon / Kim Woo-jin
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 mixed team gold medalists; both individual stars.

potentialContenders.name: Brady Ellison / TBD
potentialContenders.note: Ellison could pair with a USA women's qualifier; home venue advantage.

ARC13.blurb:
Mixed team recurve medals are decided tonight — quarterfinals through gold. Lim Si-hyeon and Kim Woo-jin won Paris gold for South Korea and would enter as heavy favorites if they compete. One man, one woman, alternating shots, elimination pressure on every arrow.

USA could field Brady Ellison in mixed team, bringing four Games of experience to a home final. Compound mixed team debuted earlier in the week; this is recurve's turn.

Evening at Carson Stadium — lights, crowd, and the unique rhythm of mixed pairs archery where strategy runs between arrows.
ARC13.potentialContendersIntro: Expected mixed team pairs competing for recurve medals — Paris champions and projected contenders.

potentialContenders.name: Lim Si-hyeon / Kim Woo-jin
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 mixed team gold; both also Paris individual medalists.

potentialContenders.name: Brady Ellison / TBD

ARC14.blurb:
Men's recurve individual reaches round of 16 — the bracket is down to eight matches, head-to-head, best of five sets. Lee Woo-seok won Paris individual gold; Kim Woo-jin took silver. If both compete for Korea, the draw could feature an all-Korea semifinal.

Brady Ellison, if he qualifies, would be shooting in front of a home crowd in his likely final Games. Round of 16 is where contenders separate from qualifiers.

Thursday late morning at Carson Stadium — single-elimination archery at its sharpest, every arrow deciding who advances.

ARC15.blurb:
Men's recurve individual medals — quarterfinals, semifinals, bronze, and gold all in one night. Lee Woo-seok won Paris gold; Kim Woo-jin took silver. If both make the semis, Korea could face itself for gold. Brady Ellison, if competing, seeks the home medal that's eluded him across four prior Games.

Archers shoot one arrow at a time in the final — no sets, just points. Maximum pressure, dead silence between shots, and the gold decided by millimeters.

Thursday evening at Carson Stadium — lights, crowd filling every seat, and the quietest tension in all of sport when an archer draws.

ARC16.blurb:
The recurve women's individual bracket narrows to eight survivors at Carson Stadium. Elimination rounds in archery are one-on-one duels — five sets, first to six points, sudden death if tied.

South Korea's assembly line of champions will likely place multiple archers in this round. Lim Si-hyeon, the Paris 2024 gold medalist, could appear if she qualifies for the 2028 team through Korea's notoriously cutthroat trials.

Carson Stadium sits at Dignity Health Sports Park, a purpose-built venue for the Games. Archers shoot from 70 meters — the targets look impossibly small from the stands, and every arrow lands in near-silence.
ARC16.potentialContendersIntro: Lim Si-hyeon won gold in Paris, but South Korea's selection process is ruthless — no spot is guaranteed four years out. An San, the Tokyo triple gold medalist, failed to make the Paris team.

ARC17.blurb:
Four medal matches unfold in a single electric evening at Carson Stadium. Quarterfinals cut eight archers to four, semifinals determine the gold medal matchup, and the bronze and gold finals close the night under lights.

This is archery's highest-stakes format — one arrow at a time, maximum pressure, the crowd holding its breath between shots. South Korea has owned this event for decades, but single-elimination brackets produce upsets.

The session runs late into the Southern California night. Carson Stadium's temporary stands put you close to the shooting line — close enough to hear the snap of the bowstring and the thud of arrows hitting targets 70 meters downrange.
ARC17.potentialContendersIntro: Lim Si-hyeon enters as the reigning Paris champion, though An San's absence from Paris shows how uncertain South Korean team selection can be. Expect at least two Korean archers projected to reach the medal rounds.

ATH01.blurb:
Track and field opens at the Coliseum — the same stadium where Carl Lewis ran in 1984. Morning prelims bring the first glimpse of the world's fastest sprinters in the 100m heats, plus qualifying rounds in shot put, hammer throw, and pole vault.

The session includes over 80 athletes across six events. Sprinters jockey for semifinal spots; throwers chase the top-12 qualifying mark. Early rounds are loose and exploratory — athletes finding rhythm before the stakes rise.

You're watching from the rim of a stadium that has hosted Games twice before. The temporary track sits elevated on stilts, and 68,000 seats wrap around the oval in a configuration unchanged since Jesse Owens's era.
ATH01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Julien Alfred, Sha'Carri Richardson, and Noah Lyles are expected to appear in the 100m heats, along with hammer thrower Annette Echikunwoke and pole vaulter Mondo Duplantis.

ATH02.blurb:
The women's 100m final is the first sprint medal event of the Games. Julien Alfred of Saint Lucia defends her Paris 2024 gold; Sha'Carri Richardson returns for redemption. The race lasts ten seconds — the buildup lasts years.

The session also crowns the first throwing champion in the shot put final and closes with the men's 10,000m, a 25-lap endurance test. Evening sessions at the Coliseum carry a different energy — cooler air, sharper times, louder crowds.

You're in the stadium where Flo-Jo set her world record. The sunset lights the peristyle arches gold, and 68,000 voices rise as the gun goes up.
ATH02.potentialContendersIntro: The women's 100m final is expected to feature Paris 2024 medalists Julien Alfred, Sha'Carri Richardson, and Melissa Jefferson. Shot put could see Yemisi Ogunleye and Maddi Wesche return.

ATH03.blurb:
The decathlon begins — ten events over two days to crown the world's greatest athlete. Day one: 100m, long jump, shot put, high jump, 400m. Morning sessions are where decathletes establish early leads or fall too far behind to recover.

Simultaneously, shot putters and long jumpers compete in qualifying. Ryan Crouser, the two-time defending champion, appears in shot put qualifying. Miltiadis Tentoglou, back-to-back Games long jump gold medalist, starts his title defense.

The Coliseum floor is a grid of simultaneous competitions. Decathletes sprint while throwers spin in the ring. It's organized chaos at track's highest level.
ATH03.potentialContendersIntro: Ryan Crouser and Joe Kovacs of USA are projected to dominate shot put qualifying. Miltiadis Tentoglou enters long jump as the two-time defending champion.

ATH04.blurb:
The men's 100m final — the single fastest race in sport. Noah Lyles defends his Paris gold on home soil. Kishane Thompson of Jamaica and Letsile Tebogo of Botswana could challenge. The race is over in under ten seconds; the noise lasts longer.

The session also awards medals in shot put, where Ryan Crouser chases an unprecedented third consecutive Games gold, and crowns the mixed 4x400m relay champions in a young, electric event.

Evening at the Coliseum. The stands shake. Carl Lewis won four golds here in 1984, and every sprint final since has carried that ghost.
ATH04.potentialContendersIntro: The men's 100m final is expected to feature Paris 2024 medalists Noah Lyles, Kishane Thompson, and Fred Kerley. Ryan Crouser is the overwhelming favorite in shot put.

ATH05.blurb:
Decathlon day two begins — 110m hurdles, discus, pole vault still ahead. Every event matters; every foul erases points. The women's triple jump final awards the session's only individual medal, where Jasmine Moore of USA could challenge on home ground.

Pole vault qualifying runs concurrently. Mondo Duplantis, the world record holder and two-time champion, begins his quest for a third gold. Qualifying is a formality for him; the bar rarely drops below his warm-up height.

Morning at the Coliseum. The track is busy, the crowd still arriving. Decathletes grind through rounds while specialists chase their singular event.
ATH05.potentialContendersIntro: The women's triple jump final could feature Paris 2024 medalists Thea LaFond, Shanieka Ricketts, and Jasmine Moore. Mondo Duplantis is expected to breeze through pole vault qualifying.

ATH06.blurb:
Mondo Duplantis defends his pole vault title in front of a sunset crowd. He has won the last two Games golds and continues to push the world record higher. The bar rises; the stadium holds its breath.

The decathlon concludes with the 1500m — a brutal closer that separates the truly great from the merely excellent. Two days, ten events, one champion. The mixed 4x100m relay makes its final debut.

Evening light slants across the Coliseum. The decathlon 1500m is pure survival — legs dead, lungs burning, crowds roaring them home.

ATH07.blurb:
Qualification rounds set the stage for finals later in the week. Women's pole vaulters chase the top-12 mark; Katie Moon and Nina Kennedy, who shared gold in a previous world championship, could appear. The men's 110m hurdles heats bring the first look at the technical sprint event.

The 3000m steeplechase — water jumps, barriers, and tactical chaos — begins its qualifying rounds. The 400m rounds sort the one-lap sprinters. Morning sessions are workmanlike, athletes saving energy for what's ahead.

The Coliseum in mid-week. Fewer crowds, more space. Athletes fine-tune in rounds that matter but don't yet medal.

ATH08.blurb:
Three finals in one session: high jump, discus, and the women's 5000m. The 5000m is a long-distance slugfest that explodes in the final lap. Faith Kipyegon and Beatrice Chebet of Kenya could duel for gold.

The women's 800m final crowns the two-lap champion. Keely Hodgkinson of Great Britain won Paris 2024 and arrives as the favorite. The 400m hurdles semifinals run concurrently — half-lap sprints over ten barriers.

Evening at the Coliseum. Three medal ceremonies, overlapping celebrations, the crowd shifting attention between the track and the field.

ATH09.blurb:
The women's 3000m steeplechase final is the marquee event — seven and a half laps of barriers, water jumps, and oxygen debt. Steeplechase finals are messy, tactical, and thrilling. The pack stays tight until someone breaks.

Long jump qualifying and hammer throw qualifying run simultaneously. Tara Davis-Woodhall, the Paris 2024 long jump champion, could compete for USA. Repechage rounds in the 400m and 200m give second-chance qualifiers a path forward.

Mid-morning. The session is dense with events but light on spectacle compared to evening finals. Athletes grind through rounds; the real drama waits.

ATH10.blurb:
The men's long jump final could be a duel between Miltiadis Tentoglou, the two-time defending champion, and emerging challengers. Long jump finals come down to a handful of big leaps — one foul can cost a medal.

The men's 1500m final is the metric mile, a tactical masterpiece. Jakob Ingebrigtsen and Josh Kerr could renew their rivalry. The 400m hurdles final crowns the one-lap barrier king. Three individual golds in one session.

Evening at the Coliseum. The 1500m is pure drama — surges, counterattacks, a sprint finish that leaves everyone gasping.

ATH11.blurb:
The men's 3000m steeplechase final is seven and a half laps of barriers, water pits, and attrition. Steeplechasers are middle-distance runners who also happen to be hurdlers. The final lap is chaos.

High jump qualifying begins — athletes chasing the top-12 mark for the final. Yaroslava Mahuchikh of Ukraine, the Paris 2024 champion, could appear. The 800m heats bring the two-lap sprinters, including Emmanuel Wanyonyi of Kenya.

Morning at the Coliseum. Qualifying rounds stack up; the session feels like a dress rehearsal for the finals still to come.

ATH12.blurb:
Nina Kennedy of Australia defends her Paris 2024 pole vault gold. Katie Moon of USA, the silver medalist, could push her. The bar rises until one vaulter remains — high-stakes limbo at 15 feet.

The women's 400m hurdles final crowns the half-lap barrier champion. Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone and Femke Bol could renew their rivalry if both compete. The men's 200m semifinals thin the field before the final.

Evening at the Coliseum. The pole vault final stretches deep into the session — each height a drama, each miss a potential elimination.

ATH13.blurb:
The heptathlon begins — seven events over two days to crown the best all-around female athlete. Day one: 100m hurdles and high jump in the morning, shot put and 200m later. Heptathletes are versatile, relentless, and rarely famous until they win.

The men's triple jump final runs simultaneously. Jordan Díaz of Spain, the Paris 2024 champion, defends his title. Triple jump is controlled explosion — hop, step, jump into the sand.

Morning at the Coliseum. The heptathlon is a slow build; the triple jump is instant drama. Two different rhythms, one session.

ATH14.blurb:
The men's 200m final is the marquee race. Letsile Tebogo of Botswana, the Paris 2024 champion at just 22, faces Noah Lyles and Kenny Bednarek of USA. The 200m requires bend-running skill and raw speed; evening conditions favor fast times.

The men's 110m hurdles final crowns the technical sprint champion — ten barriers in under 13 seconds. The women's 10,000m final is 25 laps of endurance chess. Sifan Hassan could attempt another distance double.

Evening at the Coliseum. The 200m final is pure spectacle — the bend, the straightaway, the roar as they cross.
ATH14.potentialContendersIntro: The men's 200m final is projected to feature Paris 2024 medalists Letsile Tebogo, Kenny Bednarek, and Noah Lyles. The women's 10,000m could see Sifan Hassan if she competes.

ATH15.blurb:
Four individual sprint finals in one session — the 200m, 400m for both men and women. Gabby Thomas defends her Paris 2024 200m gold. Marileidy Paulino and Quincy Hall could return in the 400m. Four races, four champions, one afternoon.

The heptathlon concludes with the 800m — two laps that decide the all-around title after a day and a half of competition. The women's long jump final crowns the horizontal jump champion. Tara Davis-Woodhall won Paris 2024 on home soil.

Afternoon at the Coliseum. The schedule is relentless — medals awarded, anthems played, the next race already lining up.
ATH15.potentialContendersIntro: The women's 200m final is projected to feature Gabby Thomas, Julien Alfred, and Brittany Brown. The 400m finals could see Marileidy Paulino, Quincy Hall, and Matt Hudson-Smith. Long jump features Tara Davis-Woodhall.

ATH16.blurb:
The penultimate evening at the Coliseum delivers wall-to-wall finals and semifinals across nine events. Women's high jump and javelin crown champions in the field while the men's 800m final explodes in the home stretch — defending champion Emmanuel Wanyonyi leads a loaded field chasing the bell lap.

Faith Kipyegon goes for an unprecedented third straight 1500m gold in front of 68,000. The women's 100m hurdles semifinals set the final field for Monday's showdown. The men's 4x100m relay final closes the night with sub-10 speed and handoff chaos.

Sunday evening under the Coliseum lights is pure track theater. The temporary track sits on stilts raising the floor to 1984 levels, the same surface where Carl Lewis made history. Relay prelims for both 4x400m squads bookend the session — USA teams could own this venue.
ATH16.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions anchor the medal conversation. Kipyegon chases history in the 1500m, Wanyonyi defends in the 800m, and Mahuchikh leads a stacked high jump field.

ATH17.blurb:
The final night of track and field at the Coliseum. Six finals crown the last champions of the 2028 Games, including the men's 5000m endurance battle and both sprint relay showcases. The women's 4x100m final could see USA defend on home soil with a crowd desperate to explode.

The men's javelin final and women's 100m hurdles final add field and sprint gold to the medal haul. Both 4x400m relay finals close the session — mixed-gender relay chaos meets pure speed as the Coliseum says goodbye to another generation of athletes.

This is the last chance to witness track and field in a venue hosting its third Games across nearly a century. The temporary track will be dismantled after Monday night. When the final relay anchor crosses the line, the Coliseum exhales.

ATH18.blurb:
The half-marathon race walk makes its Olympic debut at LA28, replacing the 20km distance in a high-stakes endurance test where technique matters as much as speed. One illegal stride caught by the judges means a warning; three warnings and you're disqualified no matter how far ahead you are.

Both men's and women's finals unfold over the same morning session, with walkers navigating LA's streets before finishing on the hallowed grounds of the LA Memorial Coliseum. The early start beats the California heat but demands athletes peak at dawn.

Race walking rewards fans willing to wake up early with raw tactical drama — watch athletes push the edge of legal form while managing their pace over 13.1 miles, knowing one misstep ends their Olympic dream.
ATH18.potentialContendersIntro: Ecuador's Brian Daniel Pintado and Spain's Alvaro Martin took gold and bronze respectively in the 20km at Paris 2024, while China's Yang Jiayu holds the world record at that distance and Spain's Maria Perez is a world champion. All four could appear in LA if they successfully transition to the new half-marathon format.

ATH19.blurb:
The women's marathon launches at 7:15 AM from Venice Beach, threading 26.2 miles through LA's neighborhoods before the iconic finish inside the Coliseum. This is one of the most-watched events of any Olympics, a grueling test of endurance and tactics that crowns one of the Games' most celebrated champions.

Paris gold medalist Sifan Hassan and world record holder Tigst Assefa could headline a world-class field if they compete. The early start beats the heat; the course showcases LA's diversity from beach to boulevards to the historic stadium bowl.

You'll catch the finish inside the Coliseum, where marathoners complete their final lap on the track. The roar as athletes enter the tunnel and emerge onto the oval is one of the great Olympic moments—exhaustion, triumph, and history converging in a single sprint to the line.
ATH19.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champion Sifan Hassan and world record holder Tigst Assefa are projected contenders if they qualify, along with Kenya's deep marathon tradition likely sending multiple medal threats.

ATH20.blurb:
The men's marathon begins at Venice Beach at 7:15 AM Sunday, the final athletics event of LA28. Runners will cover 26.2 miles through iconic LA neighborhoods before finishing inside the Coliseum, where the Olympic marathon tradition meets one of America's most storied stadiums.

Paris champion Tamirat Tola could defend his title if he competes, facing projected challenges from Kenya's marathon powerhouses and a deep international field. The early start provides cooler temperatures; the route—still partially under wraps—promises a tour of the city's character from oceanfront to urban core.

Your seat inside the Coliseum puts you at the climax: marathoners completing their final lap on the track after three hours of racing. The finish-line emotion, the crowd's thunder as runners enter the stadium—it's the ultimate closing act for Olympic track and field.

BDM01.blurb:
The Games open with badminton's group stage — a morning marathon showcasing all five Olympic events at once across multiple courts. Players jockey for knockout-round positioning through best-of-three-games pool matches, where every point shapes the bracket.

Shuttlecocks blur at 300+ mph as Asian powerhouses and rising nations clash in doubles and singles. Early sessions offer an accessible entry point to see the world's fastest racquet sport up close.

Galen Center's converted arena creates an intimate badminton theater. Multiple courts run simultaneously — choose your match, feel the smash echoes, watch rallies unfold from courtside angles impossible on TV.

An afternoon doubles showcase at USC Sports Center — women's pairs and mixed duos battle through group play alongside women's singles. The mixed doubles draws feature badminton's most creative shot-making: net kills, deceptive flicks, rallies that zigzag from forecourt to baseline.

China's Zheng Siwei and Huang Yaqiong, Paris mixed doubles champions, are likely to appear if they qualify. An Se-young could headline the women's singles pools as defending Olympic champion.

The converted venue packs courts tightly. Hear the shuttle crack off strings, watch doubles teams communicate in split-second gestures, feel the tactical chess match accelerate.

Viktor Axelsen, the two-time Olympic men's singles champion, could appear in this session if he qualifies. Best-of-three games to 21 points mean tight matches stretch into dramatic third-game scenarios.

Galen Center transforms into a multi-court arena. Evening start times draw local crowds; the atmosphere builds as matches overlap and upsets brew across simultaneous courts.

BDM20.blurb:
An epic evening session: men's singles quarterfinals narrow the field to four, while men's doubles semifinals decide who plays for gold. Seven hours of elite badminton under one roof.

Viktor Axelsen could face his toughest test yet if he reaches the quarterfinals — expect Asian challengers like Kunlavut Vitidsarn or China's rising stars. Doubles semifinals will showcase the world's most synchronized pairs, executing drop shots and smashes with surgical precision.

Galen Center settles into a rhythm. Between matches, you hear the thwack of practice rallies from adjacent courts, the murmur of strategy sessions in a dozen languages.

BDM21.blurb:
Women's doubles decides its champions in one concentrated morning. Gold and bronze medal matches play out back-to-back — three games to 21, best-of-three, winner takes the podium.

Chinese pairs won gold at Paris 2024 and typically dominate this event, but South Korean and Japanese teams have broken through in recent World Championships. Doubles rewards anticipation — the best pairs cut off shots before opponents realize they've hit them.

Galen Center on a Saturday morning fills with fans who traveled from across the Pacific. The arena echoes with coordinated cheers, flags waving in unison at match point.

BDM22.blurb:
Four women's singles quarterfinals decide who advances to the medal round. Each match is a war of attrition — long rallies test endurance, deceptive drop shots punish overeager footwork.

An Se-young is projected to defend her Paris 2024 gold if she qualifies for LA28. China's deep bench and emerging Southeast Asian players could produce surprise semifinalists.

Evening sessions at Galen Center carry a different intensity. The crowd grows louder as the night wears on, every point amplified by the enclosed arena acoustics.

BDM23.blurb:
Men's doubles crowns its champion while men's singles semifinals set up the final. The doubles gold medal match is pure entertainment — pairs diving for saves, executing around-the-head smashes, celebrating points with synchronized roars.

Viktor Axelsen could be one semifinal away from defending his title if he advances. Expect tactical chess matches at this stage — players who've studied each other for years, probing for the one weakness that cracks under Olympic pressure.

Galen Center on a Sunday morning. Medal ceremonies interrupt the action, national anthems echoing off the rafters while the next match warms up on an adjacent court.

BDM24.blurb:
Two critical matches: women's singles semifinals decide who plays for gold, and men's doubles bronze is awarded. The women's semis are the night's headliners — this is where Olympic dreams either survive or end in tears.

An Se-young could face her toughest opponent yet if she reaches this stage. China typically fields multiple semifinalists in women's singles, setting up potential intra-squad battles for a spot in the final.

Galen Center late at night has a gladiatorial feel. The crowd thins to hardcore fans, the stakes unmistakable — win and you're one match from a medal, lose and you go home empty-handed.

BDM25.blurb:
Badminton's signature event: men's singles gold and bronze medals decided in one morning. The final is a physical and mental grind — rallies exceed 30 shots, players sprint corner to corner for 45 minutes, every deception attempt catalogued and countered.

Viktor Axelsen is projected to defend his title if he qualifies and reaches the final. Kunlavut Vitidsarn could represent Thailand's best shot at gold in a generation. Whoever wins will have survived a week-long gauntlet of the world's best.

Galen Center for an Olympic final feels different than a prelim. Every seat filled, every point contested like match point, the medal ceremony afterward drenched in emotion.

BDM26.blurb:
Badminton's final event: women's singles gold and bronze medals under the lights. The champion will have won six matches in eight days, outlasting the deepest field in Olympic badminton.

An Se-young could defend her Paris 2024 title if she qualifies and advances — no confirmation yet of her LA28 status. China's depth means multiple athletes could reach this stage, making the final a toss-up until the last point.

Galen Center on closing night. The crowd knows this is the last badminton match of LA28, the final chance to hear that sharp crack of shuttle on racquet echoing through an Olympic arena.

BK301.blurb:
The first outdoor 3x3 session of the Games opens the tournament. This is streetball at the Olympics — half-court, first to 21, ten-minute sprint format with a 12-second shot clock.

With the field expanded to 12 teams per gender (up from 8 in Paris), early pool games set the table for knockout rounds. USA enters as host, while Latvia's men and Germany's women return as defending champions.

Valley Complex 3 sits open-air in Sepulveda Basin. The octagonal layout puts you right on top of the action, no matter where you sit.
BK301.potentialContendersIntro: Latvia and Germany won gold in Paris; USA is guaranteed a spot as host and could field strong squads given home-court advantage and deep streetball roots.

BK302.blurb:
Four more pool games under the Valley sky as the 3x3 tournament unfolds. The evening session brings cooler air and the chance to watch world-class half-court hoops in a format born on playgrounds.

Qualification won't be finalized until mid-2028, but expect top FIBA-ranked federations and emerging nations gunning for upset wins. The expanded 12-team bracket means more variety in matchups.

The outdoor octagon at Valley Complex 3 feels intimate — you're close enough to hear the trash talk.
BK302.potentialContendersIntro: Latvia's men and Germany's women are projected contenders based on Paris success; USA will compete on home soil.

BK303.blurb:
Late-night 3x3 under the lights. Two men's games, two women's games, all half-court intensity as pool play continues into Thursday night.

The 12-team field is still forming — six spots per gender go to qualifying tournaments in spring 2028, five to top-ranked federations by December 2027. This session could feature established powers or hungry underdogs who punched their ticket late.

Night sessions at Valley Complex 3 have a pickup-game energy. The temporary venue was built for this — all sightlines, no bad seats.
BK303.potentialContendersIntro: Latvia and Germany return as Paris gold medalists. USA is guaranteed entry as host and could emerge as a medal threat with home support.

BK304.blurb:
Day two of pool rounds. Four games across both tournaments as the 12-team brackets begin to take shape.

Half the field won't be decided until qualifying wraps in June 2028, but the afternoon slot is accessible — a low-risk entry point to watch the format if you've never seen Olympic 3x3 live. Expect fast breaks, physical defense, and games decided in the final possession.

The outdoor setting at Valley Complex 3 matches the sport's roots. You're watching the NBA's streetball cousin in its natural habitat.
BK304.potentialContendersIntro: Paris champions Latvia (men) and Germany (women) are projected to return if they qualify; USA fields teams as host nation.

BK305.blurb:
Evening pool play continues at Valley Complex 3. The format is relentless — two games per gender, every possession critical in a race to 21.

By late 2027, half the field per gender will be locked in through federation rankings. The rest earn their way via March-June 2028 qualifiers, meaning this session could showcase both established giants and Cinderella squads who clawed through the gauntlet.

Sunset timing, open air, and a crowd that knows the game. This is 3x3 as intended.
BK305.potentialContendersIntro: Latvia men and Germany women won Paris gold; USA is guaranteed spots and expected to contend on home turf.

BK306.blurb:
Prime-time 3x3 to close out Monday. Four pool games under lights as the tournament grinds through its preliminary phase.

The women's bracket is wide open after Germany's shock Paris gold — expect competitive chaos. Latvia's men have owned the format for years, but the expanded field and home-court USA squad make the men's side equally unpredictable.

Valley Complex 3's octagonal design means every seat is courtside. Late sessions feel like a block party with an Olympic scoreboard.
BK306.potentialContendersIntro: Defending champions Latvia (men) and Germany (women) could appear if they qualify; USA teams are confirmed as host nation entrants.

BK307.blurb:
Midweek pool play as the field narrows. Two men's games, two women's games, each a ten-minute war of attrition on the half-court.

LATVIA's men are the team to beat — Paris gold, years of dominance, and Nauris Miezis anchoring the roster if he returns. On the women's side, Germany stunned everyone in 2024; we'll see if they can repeat.

The Sepulveda Basin setting is pure LA — outdoor hoops in the Valley, with mountains in the distance and the crowd spilling noise onto the court.

BK316.blurb:
Four play-in games kick off 3x3 basketball at LA28, deciding which lower-seeded teams advance to the quarterfinals. The Valley Complex octagonal court puts you right on top of the action — outdoor streetball energy under the California sun.

USA's men and women are guaranteed spots as hosts; the rest of the field won't be locked until mid-2028 through world rankings and qualifying tournaments. These play-ins separate contenders from pretenders in fast, physical half-court games to 21.

The open-air setup channels the blacktop roots of 3x3. Expect close sightlines, DJ-driven atmosphere, and the crack of the ball on concrete.
BK316.potentialContendersIntro: The play-in field is unknown until 2028 qualifiers conclude, but expect gritty teams fighting for quarterfinal berths against higher seeds.

BK317.blurb:
The first four quarterfinals — two men's, two women's — narrow the field of 12 down to medal contenders. Latvia's men defended gold in Paris; Germany shocked the world on the women's side. Both could be in the mix if they qualify.

USA's men and women will compete on home concrete, guaranteed entry as hosts. Half the field will be set by December 2027 rankings; the rest arrive via spring 2028 qualifiers. Quarterfinals are win-or-go-home.

Valley Complex 3's octagonal design means no bad seats — you're surrounded by the court, feeling every cut and screen.
BK317.potentialContendersIntro: Projected contenders based on recent form — exact rosters finalize through late 2027 rankings and 2028 qualifying tournaments.

BK318.blurb:
The second set of quarterfinals wraps up Friday night under lights, finishing what BK317 started. Four more teams punch semifinal tickets; four go home. The half-court format rewards toughness and shot-making — every possession counts in 10-minute sprints.

USA's squads will be hunting medals on home turf. Latvia's men could return if their 3x3 dynasty extends to LA28; Germany's women remain dangerous if they qualify. The field locks between December 2027 and June 2028.

Night hoops in the Valley — open air, portable bleachers tight around the octagonal court, and the crowd close enough to feel the impact.
BK318.potentialContendersIntro: Teams expected to compete based on world rankings and Paris 2024 results — lineups confirmed through late 2027 and spring 2028 qualifiers.

BK319.blurb:
Saturday evening semifinals decide who plays for medals. Four games, two per gender — win and you're guaranteed hardware. Latvia's men have owned Olympic 3x3; Germany's women pulled off the Paris upset. If both qualify, expect them here.

USA enters with host-nation spots and the streetball credibility of LA behind them. The expanded 12-team format means deeper fields than Paris. Semifinals are best-of-nothing: first to 21 or survive 10 minutes.

Valley Complex at golden hour — outdoor court, mountain backdrop, and the energy of a home crowd watching Team USA chase finals.
BK319.potentialContendersIntro: Projected semifinalists based on recent dominance and likely qualifiers — final rosters confirmed by mid-2028.

BK320.blurb:
Four medal games close out 3x3 basketball — bronze and gold for men, bronze and gold for women. Latvia's men are the dynasty to beat if they return; Germany's women proved anything can happen. USA could deliver home gold under lights.

The format is pure: half-court, 10 minutes, first to 21 wins. No timeouts, no second chances. Bronze games set the stage; gold medal games finish it. The field expands to 12 teams per gender at LA28, the deepest Olympic 3x3 tournament yet.

Saturday night at Valley Complex 3 — outdoor hoops, medals on the line, and the Valley crowd watching history on a portable court built for one weekend.
BK320.potentialContendersIntro: Likely finalists based on Paris results and projected qualifiers — exact matchups depend on 2028 qualification outcomes.

BKB01.blurb:
The women's basketball tournament opens with a doubleheader showcasing the next generation of global hoops talent. Two group phase matchups set the tone for a field chasing Team USA, who enter pursuit of an unprecedented eighth consecutive Olympic gold.

Intuit Dome — the NBA's most advanced arena — makes its Olympic debut with steep seating that puts every fan close to the action. The Halo Board overhead and LA's basketball-obsessed energy create an atmosphere unlike any preliminary round you've seen.
BKB01.potentialContendersIntro: USA enters as the reigning Paris 2024 champion. France and Australia are projected contenders based on recent World Cup and Olympic form.

Prime-time group phase basketball in Inglewood. One game, winner takes momentum into the tournament's middle rounds.

The session likely features a marquee matchup — perhaps Team USA testing an emerging challenger, or a rivalry rematch from Paris. Group stage results matter: seeding determines knockout bracket paths, and no team wants to face the Americans early.

Intuit Dome at night is a spectacle. The arena's lighting, sightlines, and The Wall — a raucous standing-room section — turn a prelim into an event.

This session could feature an underdog trying to steal a result against a medal favorite, or two mid-tier powers battling for tournament positioning. The Intuit Dome crowd, primed by two earlier games, will be fully dialed in.

The arena's intimacy makes every possession feel urgent. You're watching Olympic basketball in the building that redefined what a modern arena can be.

Luka Doncic carries Slovenia like no player carries a national team; Giannis might lead Greece into medal contention; Canada's golden generation is rising fast. These games are cagey, defensive, and decided in the final two minutes — Olympic basketball at its most intense.

Intuit Dome at night is a spectacle. The Halo Board illuminates the bowl, the crowd noise reverberates, and the intimacy of the sightlines makes every play feel like it's happening in your lap. This is LA hoops on the Olympic stage.

France earned silver in Paris and could meet the US here in a rematch. Australia claimed bronze and brings WNBA firepower across the lineup. Belgium, Serbia, and Canada are all rising programs that qualified and could contend.

Intuit Dome's state-of-the-art design wraps you in the action. Steep seating, the massive Halo Board overhead, and acoustics built for basketball — this is a semifinal atmosphere in the most advanced arena in the sport.

Intuit Dome at night is electric. The Halo Board glows above the court, the steep seating creates a cauldron of sound, and the sightlines put you right on top of every possession. This is semifinal basketball in the most modern arena on earth.

BMF01.blurb:
The qualification round sets the field for BMX Freestyle's medal session — twelve men and twelve women each get two 60-second runs to advance. Both runs count toward the score, rewarding consistency over one-hit heroics.

Paris 2024 gold medalist Jose Torres Gil could return to defend, while silver medalist Kieran Reilly and Australian veteran Natalya Diehm are projected contenders if they qualify. The field won't be finalized until mid-2028, making this an open question of who earned their spots through the Olympic Qualifier Series and UCI rankings.

Valley Complex 1 in the Sepulveda Basin trades the Venice Beach boardwalk vibe for a quieter setting, but the park ramps and transitions remain world-class. Expect huge amplitude and technical combos as riders push the envelope to secure a finals berth.
BMF01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists could appear if they qualify through the 2026-28 window, alongside emerging names from the Olympic Qualifier Series.

BMF02.blurb:
Two 60-second runs, best score counts — BMX Freestyle crowns its Olympic champions with zero margin for error. Tailwhips, backflips, 360s, and progressive combos under the California sun as riders throw their biggest tricks for gold.

If Jose Torres Gil qualifies, he'll look to repeat his Paris triumph. Kieran Reilly could challenge for the top step, while the women's field has grown deeper since Tokyo — Natalya Diehm and possibly Hannah Roberts are projected to contend. Medal outcomes hinge on who makes it through qualifying and who sticks their best run when it matters.

Valley Complex 1 hosts the finals with a dedicated BMX park setup. The crowd gets close to the ramps, and the energy peaks when a rider strings together a flawless run that sends the judges' scores soaring.

BMX01.blurb:
The quarterfinals are where Olympic dreams splinter in a cloud of dirt and twisted metal. Riders attack the Carson track's rhythm sections at full speed, navigating berms and tabletop jumps while jostling for position.

Eight riders per gate drop, three runs per quarterfinal heat — the format rewards consistency and nerve. Finish outside the top four and you're sent to the last chance qualifier, one final shot to keep your Games alive.

The Carson track sits in the Valley Complex with sightlines that put you close to the action. You'll feel the gate slam and hear the crowd roar as riders scrub speed over jumps inches from each other.

Saya Sakakibara of Australia won Paris 2024 gold after a comeback that rewrote her career. Sylvain Andre of France took men's gold on the technical Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines track. Both could return to defend their titles if they qualify and compete in LA.

The Carson BMX track features elevation changes and rhythm sections that separate technical riders from pure power. Gate-side seats put you close enough to feel the impact when riders clip wheels mid-air.

BOX01.blurb:
Day one of the LA28 boxing tournament throws open the ropes across five weight classes. Women's flyweight, lightweight, and welterweight contenders step into the ring alongside men's bantamweight, welterweight, and super heavyweight fighters — each chasing a path to the podium.

The round of 32 and round of 16 format means fast eliminations and immediate stakes. Expect crisp three-round bouts where a single misjudged exchange can end an Olympic dream.

Peacock Theater's 3,600 seats put you close enough to hear the leather connect. The matinee slot draws local crowds and families — an accessible entry point to Olympic boxing's opening salvos.
BOX01.potentialContendersIntro: Rosters are still forming, but recent world-level performances suggest who could appear. Bakhodir Jalolov, the Paris 2024 super heavyweight gold medalist, is a projected contender if Uzbekistan fields him in the +90kg bracket.

BOX02.blurb:
The opening Saturday night card mirrors the afternoon's weight classes — second chances for those who drew late bracket positions. Round of 32 and round of 16 bouts across women's 54kg, 60kg, 65kg and men's 55kg, 65kg, +90kg unfold under evening lights.

Night sessions at Peacock Theater carry different energy — louder crowds, tighter atmosphere, the kind of room where every slip and counterpunch registers. These are elimination rounds where unknown names can announce themselves.

The venue's downtown location makes this an easy post-dinner option. You'll leave knowing which fighters survived the opening gauntlet.
BOX02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists could appear if they qualify and compete at LA28. The super heavyweight bracket may feature Bakhodir Jalolov of Uzbekistan, who won gold two years prior.

BOX03.blurb:
Sunday afternoon brings a fresh slate of weights: women's flyweight and middleweight join men's lightweight, welterweight, and light heavyweight. The round of 32 thins the field; the round of 16 sharpens the contender pool.

Boxing preliminaries reward the technically sound and the tactically patient — fighters who can win on points over three rounds without taking unnecessary risks. Expect disciplined jabs, footwork, and ring generalship.

Peacock Theater's sightlines mean no bad seats. The Sunday slot draws a mix of hardcore fans tracking brackets and casual attendees discovering the sport's Olympic intensity.

BOX04.blurb:
The Sunday night closer reprises the afternoon's five weight classes with different bracket matchups. Women's 51kg and 70kg fighters step in alongside men's 60kg, 70kg, and 90kg contenders — each bout a potential upset waiting to happen.

Preliminary rounds are where Olympic narratives begin: the underdog who lands a clean counter, the favorite who stumbles, the unknown qualifier who fights like they have nothing to lose. Three rounds decide everything.

The 7 p.m. bell feels prime-time. Peacock Theater's intimacy amplifies every exchange — you'll hear the corner advice between rounds and feel the crowd sway with momentum shifts.

BOX05.blurb:
Three round-of-16 sessions compress Monday afternoon: women's featherweight, men's flyweight, and men's light welterweight. The field has narrowed — these are the fighters who survived opening weekend and now face stiffer opposition.

Round of 16 bouts carry quarterfinal implications. Win here and you're two victories from a medal. The stakes tighten the technical execution: sharper combinations, tighter defense, more calculated aggression.

Midday slots at Peacock Theater offer a quieter intensity than evening cards. Fewer tourists, more purists — the kind of crowd that appreciates a well-timed slip or a body-shot sequence.

BOX06.blurb:
Monday night mirrors the afternoon's round-of-16 action across women's 60kg, men's 55kg, and men's 65kg. The brackets are thinning fast — only sixteen remain in each weight class, and eight will advance.

Evening sessions draw the loudest crowds. Peacock Theater's acoustics turn a clean knockdown into a thunder-clap moment. These are the bouts where reputations get built or dismantled in nine minutes of ring time.

The 65kg men's bracket could feature contenders like Keyshawn Davis if the Paris 2024 silver medalist competes for the U.S. at LA28. His pro career complicates eligibility, but the storyline would be electric.
BOX06.potentialContendersIntro: Keyshawn Davis of the USA won silver in men's lightweight at Paris 2024, though his subsequent professional career raises questions about LA28 amateur eligibility. If he returns to the Olympic fold, 65kg would be his likely weight class.

BOX07.blurb:
Tuesday afternoon escalates the stakes: men's super heavyweight quarterfinals headline a card that also features women's 54kg round of 16, women's 57kg round of 32, and men's 80kg round of 16. The +90kg quarterfinals guarantee drama — four bouts, four medal spots on the line.

Super heavyweight boxing is power personified. These are the athletes who can end a fight with a single clean shot. Bakhodir Jalolov of Uzbekistan, the Paris 2024 gold medalist, is a projected contender if he qualifies and defends his title.

Quarterfinal sessions carry higher ticket prices for good reason. You're watching the moment when medal contention becomes real — win and you're guaranteed at least a bronze-medal bout.
BOX07.potentialContendersIntro: The men's +90kg quarterfinals could feature Bakhodir Jalolov, who dominated the super heavyweight division at Paris 2024. No confirmation of his LA28 participation yet, but he remains the weight class's measuring stick.

BOX08.blurb:
The Tuesday night card delivers more super heavyweight quarterfinal fireworks alongside women's 54kg and 57kg eliminations and men's 80kg round of 16. Four +90kg bouts decide the final four — the fighters who will box for gold and silver two days later.

Quarterfinals separate technical boxers from pure punchers. In the heavier weights especially, one lapse in defense can mean an early exit. The crowd knows it, the fighters know it — the tension is palpable.

Peacock Theater under lights feels like a classic boxing club scaled up for the Olympics. The 7 p.m. start draws the evening crowd — louder, more partisan, ready to roar for every big shot.
BOX08.potentialContendersIntro: Uzbekistan's Bakhodir Jalolov could appear in the +90kg quarterfinals if he qualifies. His Paris 2024 gold medal makes him the favorite, though the field won't be confirmed until closer to the Games.

BOX09.blurb:
Wednesday afternoon brings quarterfinals in women's 60kg and 70kg alongside round-of-16 action in men's 60kg and 70kg. The women's quarterfinals mean guaranteed medal bouts for the winners — four fights, four bronze-or-better finishes locked in.

Women's boxing at the Olympics has exploded in depth and talent since its modern debut. These quarterfinals will likely feature contenders from traditional powerhouses and emerging programs alike, each chasing podium history.

Midweek matinees at Peacock Theater offer a more relaxed vibe than weekend sessions. Fewer crowds mean better availability — and you still get the same up-close intensity that makes Olympic boxing unforgettable.

BOX10.blurb:
Wednesday night doubles down on quarterfinal intensity: women's 60kg and 70kg medal hopefuls return, while men's 60kg and 70kg fighters battle through the round of 16. The women's bouts decide semifinal matchups; the men's decide who advances to Thursday's quarterfinals.

The 60kg and 70kg weight classes sit in boxing's technical sweet spot — fast enough for combination punching, heavy enough for knockout power. Expect high-level ring IQ and crisp exchanges.

Evening sessions at Peacock Theater pack the energy. The venue's compact footprint means the crowd noise bounces off the walls — every big moment lands twice as hard.

BOX11.blurb:
Thursday afternoon delivers men's quarterfinals in 65kg and 90kg — guaranteed medal bouts for eight fighters — plus women's round-of-16 action in 51kg and 65kg. The men's quarterfinals decide who boxes for gold on Sunday; the women's bouts thin the path to Friday's quarterfinals.

Quarterfinal day is where Olympic boxing storylines crystallize. The bracket favorites must prove it; the underdogs get their shot. Win and you're guaranteed bronze or better — the pressure shows in every round.

Peacock Theater's sightlines bring you close to the corners. You'll see the ice bags between rounds, hear the seconds barking instructions, watch the fighters' eyes when the bell rings for round three.

BOX12.blurb:
Thursday night mirrors the afternoon's schedule: men's 65kg and 90kg quarterfinals return with different bracket matchups, while women's 51kg and 65kg fighters advance through the round of 16. Eight more men secure medal bouts; eight more women move closer.

The 65kg quarterfinals could feature American contenders if the U.S. fields a strong team in the welterweight division. The 90kg bracket showcases light heavyweights — a weight class known for explosive athleticism and tactical variety.

Night sessions draw the loudest fans. Peacock Theater's intimacy amplifies every reaction — the roar when a hometown fighter lands clean, the collective gasp when someone wobbles, the standing ovation for a warrior who leaves it all in the ring.

BOX13.blurb:
Friday afternoon stacks quarterfinals in four weight classes: women's 57kg and 75kg, men's 55kg and 80kg. Twelve bouts across the session, each worth a guaranteed bronze medal. The field has narrowed to the sport's elite — the fighters who've won every bout so far.

Quarterfinal Friday is high-stakes theater. These are the sessions where favorites can stumble and dark horses can shock the bracket. Three rounds, judges' scorecards, and the weight of a nation watching from home.

The matinee slot offers the most accessible pricing of the quarterfinal rounds. Peacock Theater fills with fans tracking multiple brackets — you'll watch history unfold across four weight divisions in one afternoon.

BOX14.blurb:
Friday night closes the quarterfinal rounds with women's 57kg and 75kg plus men's 55kg and 80kg. By the final bell, sixteen more fighters will have secured Olympic medals — the last group to punch their tickets to semifinal weekend.

The 57kg women's bracket could feature contenders from Algeria, Ireland, and beyond — nations that have built world-class programs in the lighter weights. The men's 80kg quarterfinals showcase cruiserweights, a division balanced between speed and power.

Evening quarterfinals carry an edge of finality. Win and you're two bouts from gold; lose and you settle for bronze. Peacock Theater's crowd knows the stakes — the atmosphere crackles with anticipation from the opening bell.

BOX15.blurb:
Saturday afternoon brings semifinals and quarterfinals across seven weight classes — the most loaded card of the prelims. Women's 70kg and men's super heavyweight semifinals decide Sunday's gold-medal matchups, while quarterfinals in five other weights lock in bronze-medal bouts.

The men's +90kg semifinals are destination viewing. Bakhodir Jalolov of Uzbekistan could appear if he qualifies — the Paris 2024 champion seeking a path back to the final. Super heavyweight semis deliver dramatic stakes and the sport's biggest hitters.

Peacock Theater at midday Saturday hums with bracket-watching fans. Seven weight classes mean constant action — you'll barely settle into your seat before the next bout begins. This is Olympic boxing at its most relentless.

BOX16.blurb:
Seven weight classes converge in one electric evening session — quarterfinals across four women's divisions and two men's, plus semifinals in women's 70kg and men's super heavyweight. This is where medal paths narrow and every punch carries stakes.

The men's super heavyweight semifinal could feature Uzbekistan's Bakhodir Jalolov, the Paris 2024 gold medalist, if he qualifies and advances through the bracket. Quarterfinal action in men's 60kg and 70kg will test contenders fighting for a spot in the medal rounds. The women's 70kg semifinal brings fighters within one bout of the gold medal session.

Peacock Theater turns boxing intimate — 3,600 seats mean you hear every leather-on-leather impact, every corner shout between rounds. The downtown LA energy spills into a venue where preliminary rounds feel like championship nights.

Sunday afternoon boxing delivers high-stakes quarterfinals in women's 57kg and 75kg, bracketing a men's 55kg semifinal that puts two fighters one win from the gold medal bout. Matinee sessions bring a different crowd — families, serious boxing students, LA locals treating Sunday like fight night.

The men's 55kg semifinal is where lighter-weight speed and technical precision peak under Olympic pressure. Women's quarterfinals across 57kg and 75kg span the division spectrum — flyweight agility meets light heavyweight power. Eight women fight for four semifinal spots in each weight class.

Peacock Theater at noon has different energy than evening sessions but the same brutal intimacy. Afternoon light through the venue's glass makes every bead of sweat visible from the upper rows.

An early finals session packing three semifinals and one gold medal bout into a single afternoon. Women's 70kg crowns its champion while the semifinal matchups across 51kg, 65kg, and men's 90kg set up weekend gold medal fights.

DTLA Arena's 3,600 seats put you close enough to feel the canvas shake. The intimacy amplifies every hook — this is Olympic boxing stripped to its rawest form, one ring in a small room with national pride on the line.

Arriving boxers will represent the deepest women's field in Olympic history, thanks to LA28's full gender parity with seven weight classes per gender. The 70kg final could feature Paris medalists or emerging contenders from the new qualification pathway under World Boxing.
