WPO01.blurb:
The opening session of men's water polo kicks off the Olympic tournament with two preliminary matches in a 12-team field battling for quarterfinal spots. Serbia arrives as Paris 2024 gold medalists, while the home-crowd USA squad — fresh off a bronze medal and a recent 14-12 upset of Serbia at World Cup — will look to build momentum on their way to a potential podium run.

The Long Beach Aquatics Center puts you at pool level, close enough to hear the coaches shouting and the slap of the ball on water. Early prelims are your chance to catch future medal contenders before the knockout rounds, when tickets get scarce and stakes get higher.

WPO24.blurb:
Women's semifinals under the lights — two matches separate four teams from the gold and bronze medal finals. The pool at Long Beach will be a cauldron.

Spain enters as Paris champions. The USA, three-time gold medalists this century (2012, 2016, 2020) and Paris bronze winners, are projected to contend on home soil despite their Paris semifinal loss.

Evening semifinals carry a different intensity — poolside seating means you're close enough to hear every tactical call and feel the crowd's roar echo off the water.

WPO25.blurb:
The men's bronze medal match — a single game for a place on the podium. No second chances, no consolation prizes.

The USA won Paris bronze in a penalty shootout against Hungary, breaking a 16-year medal drought. Serbia took gold. If both advance and meet again, expect fireworks — the USA beat Serbia 14-12 at the 2026 World Cup.

Long Beach's waterline seating puts you in the splash zone for one of the most physically demanding matches of the Games — bronze medal intensity, Olympic stakes.

WPO26.blurb:
The men's gold medal final — the most physical, tactically complex match in Olympic water polo. Two teams, 32 minutes, one champion.

Serbia won Paris gold and are a dynasty in the sport. The USA, bronze medalists in Paris and recent 14-12 upset winners over Serbia at the 2026 World Cup, could challenge on home soil. Hungary, the sport's most decorated nation, always looms as a threat.

Long Beach's pool-level seating puts you at waterline for the climax of the tournament — close enough to see the underwater battles and hear the roar as the final buzzer sounds.

Women's bronze medal match — one game for a place on the podium. The tournament's final act before the gold medal showdown.

The USA won Paris bronze after losing their semifinal to Spain, the eventual champions. Spain enters LA28 as defending gold medalists. If both advance and meet again, it's a rematch worth watching.

Long Beach's intimate pool setup amplifies the stakes — waterline seating puts you close enough to feel the tension as two teams fight for Olympic hardware.

The women's gold medal final — the climax of the LA28 water polo tournament. Two teams, 32 minutes, Olympic immortality.

Long Beach's waterline seating puts you in the thick of the action — close enough to see the tactical battles, hear the crowd's roar, and witness the coronation of Olympic champions.

WRE01.blurb:
Opening day at the mat — three weight classes begin their Olympic campaigns in this morning session. Men's Greco-Roman 60kg and 130kg bring the lightest and heaviest grapplers in the discipline, while Women's Freestyle 50kg features one of the sport's most dominant athletes.

Yui Susaki of Japan — the Paris 2024 champion who's lost just once in her career — is projected to compete at 50kg, making this session a chance to watch near-perfection on the mat. The Greco-Roman brackets start wide open, with 16 wrestlers per class battling through to the quarterfinals.

LA Convention Center Hall 2 puts you right on top of the action. Mat-level seating creates an intimate, almost gladiatorial atmosphere — you'll hear every breath, every coach's shout, every impact.
WRE01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists are expected to return, with Japan's Yui Susaki a near-lock at 50kg if she competes.

potentialContenders.name: Yui Susaki
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold, W Freestyle 50kg; lost once in career

WRE02.blurb:
The field narrows to four per weight class in this evening session — three semifinals across two Greco-Roman divisions and one women's freestyle bracket. Every match is one win from the medal round.

Greco-Roman wrestling allows no leg attacks, making upper-body throws and par terre ground work the whole game. The 60kg and 130kg classes represent wrestling's extremes — speed and technique versus raw power and leverage. If Yui Susaki makes the 50kg semis as projected, expect a technical clinic.

Prime-time wrestling at the Convention Center brings the crowd energy. The mat's right there — close enough to see the grip fighting, the chess match between holds.
WRE02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions are likely to reach this stage if they compete, though LA28 rosters remain unconfirmed.

potentialContenders.name: Yui Susaki

WRE03.blurb:
Day two brings two more Greco-Roman weight classes and a new women's freestyle division to the mat. The session also features repechage rounds — wrestlers who lost to finalists get a second shot at bronze.

Men's Greco-Roman 77kg and 97kg sit in the middle of the weight spectrum, where technique and power balance out. Women's Freestyle 53kg is one class up from where Yui Susaki dominates — expect a deep, competitive field. The repechages for Day 1 events mean potential medal matches are on the line even in the morning.

The Convention Center's acoustics amplify every reaction. When a wrestler executes a five-point throw, the entire hall erupts — you're part of the roar.
WRE03.potentialContendersIntro: No LA28 qualifiers confirmed yet for these weight classes; expect Paris 2024 medalists and world champions to project into the field.

WRE04.blurb:
Three Olympic champions will be crowned tonight — Men's Greco-Roman 60kg and 130kg, and Women's Freestyle 50kg. The session also features semifinals at 77kg and 97kg Greco-Roman and 53kg women's freestyle.

The 50kg women's final could be Yui Susaki's coronation if she competes — the Japanese star is virtually unbeaten and won Paris 2024 gold. The Greco-Roman finals bookend the weight classes: flyweights and super-heavyweights, finesse versus raw power. Expect five medal matches over nearly four hours.

Medal sessions at the Convention Center are wrestling's biggest stage in LA. The crowd knows what's at stake — every point, every scramble, every anthem played after.
WRE04.potentialContendersIntro: Three weight classes award medals tonight, with Paris 2024 champions projected to contend if they compete.

potentialContenders.name: Yui Susaki
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold, W Freestyle 50kg; expected to reach final

potentialContenders.name: Kenyu Yamamoto

WRE05.blurb:
Two new Greco-Roman brackets and a women's freestyle class hit the mat on Wednesday morning. Men's 67kg and 87kg begin their Olympic runs alongside Women's 57kg, with Day 2 repechages mixed in.

Paris 2024 saw Iran's Mohammadmohdi Geraei take 67kg gold, Kazakhstan's Yusup Batirmurzaev claim 87kg, and Japan's Tsugumi Sakurai win 57kg women's freestyle — all three weight classes feature returning champions if they qualify for LA. The repechage rounds give wrestlers a lifeline: lose to a finalist, and you're still alive for bronze.

The morning crowd at the Convention Center is wrestling-savvy. They know the sport's rhythms — when to gasp at a reversal, when to roar for a takedown.
WRE05.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions are expected to return in these weight classes, though LA28 rosters remain unconfirmed.

WRE06.blurb:
Three more Olympic champions crowned — Men's Greco-Roman 77kg and 97kg, Women's Freestyle 53kg. Semifinals at 67kg and 87kg Greco-Roman and 57kg women's freestyle fill out the card.

The 77kg and 97kg Greco-Roman finals could feature explosive throws and dramatic par terre sequences — these middle-to-upper weights balance technique with serious power. Five medal matches over the session mean anthem ceremonies every half hour. The semifinals set Thursday's medal round.

Prime-time wrestling brings the Convention Center to life. The crowd's close enough to lock eyes with athletes between periods — you're inside the sport, not watching from afar.
WRE06.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists in these weight classes could return for LA28, though no qualifiers are confirmed as of April 2026.

WRE07.blurb:
Freestyle wrestling arrives — both styles on display now. Men's Freestyle 57kg and 86kg allow leg attacks and a faster, more open game than Greco-Roman. Women's Freestyle 62kg joins them for Round of 16 and quarterfinals.

Freestyle changes the visual entirely: double-legs, ankle picks, scrambles to the edge of the mat. The 57kg class is lightning-fast; 86kg brings size and athleticism together. Day 3 repechages run alongside the new brackets, keeping Day 2 medalists' dreams alive.

The Convention Center crowd adjusts to freestyle's pace — more movement, more scoring, more sudden exchanges. The sport's two disciplines feel like different games played on the same mat.
WRE07.potentialContendersIntro: No LA28 qualifiers announced yet for Men's Freestyle 57kg or 86kg or Women's Freestyle 62kg; expect world-ranked wrestlers to emerge closer to the Games.

WRE08.blurb:
Three Olympic titles decided — Men's Greco-Roman 67kg and 87kg, Women's Freestyle 57kg. Semifinals in men's freestyle 57kg and 86kg and women's 62kg build toward Friday's finals.

The 57kg women's final could feature Japan's Tsugumi Sakurai if she qualifies — she won Paris 2024 gold. The Greco-Roman medals cap two weight classes that saw Iran and Kazakhstan dominate in Paris. Five medal matches, five anthems, five champions.

Evening sessions pack the Convention Center. Wrestling fans know the stakes — these are the matches athletes train entire Olympiads to reach.
WRE08.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions are projected to contend in the 67kg and 87kg Greco-Roman finals and 57kg women's freestyle if they compete.

WRE09.blurb:
Friday morning brings Men's Freestyle 74kg — one of wrestling's most contested weight classes in Olympic history. The 125kg super-heavyweights and Women's 68kg round out the session, with Day 4 repechages mixed in.

The 74kg division has seen legends like Jordan Burroughs compete, though the American star's LA28 status is uncertain at age 39. These are the weight classes where freestyle's full arsenal shows: speed, power, scrambling, edge work. Sixteen wrestlers per bracket means the mat stays hot all session.

The Convention Center's mat-level sight lines make you feel like a cornerman. You see the strategy, the adjustments, the fatigue — everything the TV cameras miss.
WRE09.potentialContendersIntro: No LA28 rosters confirmed, but 74kg is historically deep. Jordan Burroughs' participation remains uncertain.

WRE10.blurb:
Three freestyle champions crowned — Men's 57kg and 86kg, Women's 62kg. Semifinals at 74kg, 125kg men's, and 68kg women's set up Saturday's finals. This is freestyle's showcase night.

The 74kg class has produced Olympic drama for decades — if Jordan Burroughs qualifies at 39, this session sets his potential medal round. The 57kg and 86kg finals contrast styles: flyweight speed versus middleweight power. Five medal matches, coast-to-coast wrestling.

Prime-time Friday at the Convention Center. The crowd knows they're watching history — every finalist will be an Olympic champion by night's end.
WRE10.potentialContendersIntro: Freestyle medals are on the line in three weight classes, with 74kg semifinals potentially featuring Jordan Burroughs if he competes.

WRE11.blurb:
Saturday morning features Men's Freestyle 97kg — Abdulrashid Sadulaev's weight class. The Russian Tank now wrestles for Uzbekistan and could be seeking another Olympic gold if he competes. Men's 65kg and Women's 76kg complete the brackets.

The 97kg class is big-man freestyle at its best: athletes who can shoot doubles, throw headlocks, and scramble despite weighing over 200 pounds. Sadulaev has won two Olympic golds in this division — his presence would electrify the session. Day 5 repechages keep Thursday's medal hopes alive.

The Convention Center crowd leans in for 97kg — they know the legends who've wrestled this weight. If Sadulaev's on the mat, the entire hall watches.
WRE11.potentialContendersIntro: Men's Freestyle 97kg could feature Abdulrashid Sadulaev (Uzbekistan), a two-time Olympic gold medalist, though his LA28 status isn't confirmed.

WRE12.blurb:
Saturday night crowns three more freestyle champions — Men's 74kg and 125kg, Women's 68kg. Semifinals at 65kg and 97kg men's and 76kg women's set the final day's medal matches.

The 74kg final could be Jordan Burroughs' last Olympic moment if he qualifies — the American legend would be 39. The 125kg super-heavyweight final brings wrestling's biggest athletes to center mat. Five medal ceremonies, five flags raised, five dreams realized.

Evening at the Convention Center means championship atmosphere. The crowd's on their feet for every final — this is what they came for.
WRE12.potentialContendersIntro: Three freestyle finals feature potential appearances by Jordan Burroughs at 74kg and Abdulrashid Sadulaev in the 97kg semifinals, though neither is confirmed.

WRE13.blurb:
Wrestling's final session — the last three Olympic champions will be crowned. Men's Freestyle 65kg and 97kg, Women's 76kg, all with repechages leading into the medal matches.

The 97kg final could feature Abdulrashid Sadulaev chasing a third Olympic gold if he qualifies — he won Paris 2024 for Bahrain's Akhmed Tazhudinov, but the Russian Tank wrestling for Uzbekistan remains a projected force. Japan's Yuka Kagami won 76kg in Paris; Iran's Rahman Amouzad took 65kg. Sunday morning, late July, the last mat, the last matches.

The Convention Center empties after this one — wrestling leaves LA with fifteen new Olympic champions. The crowd stays through the final anthem, the final medal ceremony, the final bow.
WRE13.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions are projected to return in all three weight classes, though LA28 rosters remain unconfirmed as of April 2026.
